text.title: Load Balancer High Availability and Cloud Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production LBs MUST be deployed in an HA topology — single instances rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Cloud LBs MUST span ≥2 availability zones; single-AZ deployments rejected unless DR plan documents the choice.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Cloud LB product MUST be selected by OSI layer (ALB=L7 HTTP, NLB=L4 TCP/UDP, GLB=Global L7); mismatched products are rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Failover MUST be tested via game-day at least quarterly; untested HA is theatre.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: DNS-based failover MUST acknowledge TTL-bounded RTO; relying on it for <5-minute RTO is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when internal LB with no SLO OR single-AZ accepted DR-less workload.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
